characters_found.name: Narrator (Authorial I)
direct_quotes: What vitality! Man has conquered everything and destroyed millions of plants, yet this one won't submit.
actions: Reflects on the vitality of a plant that refuses to submit
Remembers a Caucasian episode from years ago
Frames the coming narrative as partly witnessed, partly heard from eye-witnesses, and partly imagined
inner_states: Contemplative, associative mind that moves from the plant to a remembered historical episode
Awareness that the episode has been shaped by his own memory and imagination
social_markers: Russian authorial voice with knowledge of the Caucasus

relationship_dynamics.quality: admiring, projects heroic quality of resistance onto it
mediates and frames his story; positions himself as witness/curator

new_info: Explicitly admits the episode is a hybrid of personal observation, second-hand reports, and imagination
Anchors the coming narrative in late 1851 and in the Caucasus

characters_found.name: Hadji Murad
actions: Rides into Makhmet, a hostile Chechen aoul, on a cold November evening in late 1851
Travels as a fugitive with only one murid, wrapped in hood and burka with a rifle protruding
Tries to attract as little attention as possible
Peers with quick black eyes into the faces of passers-by
Avoids the main road to the open square, turns left into a narrow side street
Stops at the second saklya, cut into the hillside, and looks round
Touches the man lying on the roof with the handle of his leather-plaited whip and clicks his tongue to rouse him
inner_states: Cautious, vigilant, aware of hostile surroundings
Calculating — deliberately dims his usual display to avoid detection
Purposeful: knows exactly which saklya he is heading for
social_markers: Shamil's naib — high-ranking Caucasian military/political figure under the Imam
Famous warrior known for his exploits
Normally travels with a banner and dozens of caracoling murids — indicator of elevated status
Muslim Caucasian (enters an aoul during/after muezzin's call, uses saklya, burka, beshmet culture)

relationship_dynamics.quality: formally his naib, but now a fugitive — relationship has fractured or become dangerous
trusted enough to be his sole companion in flight

relationship_dynamics.with: the old man on the roof
relationship_dynamics.quality: knows him / knows the house; approaches non-verbally with whip and tongue-click, suggesting familiarity or a pre-arranged contact
enters it as an enemy territory, must conceal himself

new_info: Setting anchor: late 1851, cold November evening, aoul Makhmet, ~15 miles from Russian territory
Has just become a fugitive — stripped down from naib-with-entourage to fugitive-with-one-murid
Has a specific destination and contact in Makhmet (the second saklya cut into the hillside)
Physical description detail: quick black eyes; carries rifle under burka; uses a leather-plaited whip

characters_found.name: Hadji Murad's Murid (unnamed, active)
actions: Rides alongside Hadji Murad as his sole companion into hostile Makhmet
social_markers: Murid — devoted follower in the Caucasian Muslim resistance sense
Willing to accompany his leader into enemy aoul at great personal risk

relationship_dynamics.quality: loyal sole companion in fugitive flight; the last of what was once dozens of murids

new_info: Of the former entourage of dozens of murids, only this one remains with Hadji Murad in his flight

characters_found.name: Old man on the saklya roof
actions: Lies asleep/resting on the roof of the saklya behind the freshly plastered clay chimney, covered with a sheepskin
Rises from under the sheepskin when Hadji Murad touches him with the whip handle and clicks his tongue
social_markers: Wears a greasy old beshmet and a nightcap — domestic, modest, worn clothing, suggesting humble or everyday status in the aoul
Caucasian Muslim village inhabitant (sleeps on saklya roof, beshmet)

relationship_dynamics.quality: addressee of Hadji Murad's silent, familiar summons; appears to be the contact Hadji Murad rode to meet

new_info: First physical introduction: sleeps on the roof behind the chimney under a sheepskin
Attire: greasy old beshmet and nightcap
Responds to the non-verbal summons rather than to a shout — consistent with a discreet, possibly pre-arranged meeting
